task_completion.description: Navigate to the system-wide default dashboard configuration page as an admin.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the Moodle 'Default dashboard page' configuration screen (usually under Site administration > Appearance). The user profile should indicate an admin is logged in.

task_completion.description: Remove the 'Online users' block from the default dashboard.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Verify that the 'Online users' block is no longer visible anywhere on the default dashboard configuration page.

task_completion.description: Add a 'Timeline' block to the central content region.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a block titled 'Timeline' positioned in the main/central content area of the dashboard layout.

task_completion.description: Add a 'Text' block to the side region with the title 'Student Support'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a Text/HTML block located in the side region (typically the right column) with the exact block header/title 'Student Support'.

task_completion.description: Configure the 'Student Support' block with the correct contact information.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the text 'For technical assistance, please contact helpdesk@university.edu or call 555-0199' within the content area of the 'Student Support' block.

task_completion.description: Reset the dashboard for all existing users to apply the changes.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the agent clicking the 'Reset dashboard for all users' button on the Default dashboard page, or a confirmation message indicating the reset was successful.

integrity.description: The agent used the Moodle web interface to configure the dashboard.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for a web browser navigating the Moodle UI. The agent should not be using terminal commands, database queries (like MySQL), or scripts to modify the dashboard blocks.

integrity.description: The blocks were added using Moodle's built-in block editing tools.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for Moodle's 'Add a block' menu and block configuration forms (gear icons). The agent should not be using browser developer tools to fake the visual appearance of the blocks.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual hosted Moodle application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The browser address bar should show the correct Moodle application URL (e.g., localhost or a specific domain), not a local file path (file://).

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires configuring the Moodle system-wide default dashboard. Required blocks are 'Timeline' (must be in the center region) and 'Text'/'HTML' (must be in the side region). The 'Online users' block must be completely removed. The Text block must be titled 'Student Support' and contain the email 'helpdesk@university.edu'. Finally, the agent must trigger the 'Reset dashboard for all users' function to ensure changes propagate to existing users.
